str:Agency.com:Name: SDMX
str:Agency.com:Description: SDMX is an initiative to foster standards for the exchange of statistical information. It is sponsored by the Bank for International Settlements (BIS), the European Central Bank (ECB), the Statistical Office of the European Union (Eurostat), the International Monetary Fund (IMF), the Organization for Economic Co-Operation and Development (OECD), the United Nations (UN) and the World Bank.

str:Contact.com:Name: SDMX Secretariat
str:Contact.str:Role: Single entry point for external inquiries

str:Agency.com:Name: International Monetary Fund (IMF)
str:Agency.com:Description: The IMF works to foster global growth and economic stability. It provides policy advice and financing to members in economic difficulties and also works with developing nations to help them achieve macroeconomic stability and reduce poverty.

str:Contact.com:Name: Single point of contact for external inquiries
str:Contact.str:Role: Single point of contact for external inquiries

str:Agency.com:Name: Statistical Office of the European Union (Eurostat)
str:Agency.com:Description: Eurostat is the statistical office of the European Union situated in Luxembourg. Its task is to provide the European Union with high quality statistics at European level that enable comparisons between countries and regions. Website: http://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/.

str:Agency.com:Name: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS)
str:Agency.com:Description: The UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS) is the statistical office of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO). The Institute produces the data and methodologies to monitor trends at national and international levels. It delivers comparative data for countries at all stages of development to provide a global perspective on education, science and technology, culture, and communication. The UIS is the official source for the indicators needed to achieve SDG 4–Education 2030 and key targets in science and innovation, culture and communication.

str:Contact.com:Name: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS)
str:Contact.str:Role: Single entry point for external inquiries

str:Agency.com:Name: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)
str:Agency.com:Description:
The mission of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is to promote policies that will improve the economic and social well-being of people around the world.
The OECD provides a forum in which governments can work together to share experiences and seek solutions to common problems. We work with governments to understand what drives economic, social and environmental change. We measure productivity and global flows of trade and investment. We analyse and compare data to predict future trends. We set international standards on a wide range of things, from agriculture and tax to the safety of chemicals.
We look, too, at issues that directly affect the lives of ordinary people, like how much they pay in taxes and social security, and how much leisure time they can take. We compare how different countries’ school systems are readying their young people for modern life, and how different countries’ pension systems will look after their citizens in old age.
Drawing on facts and real-life experience, we recommend policies designed to make the lives of ordinary people better. We work with business, through the Business and Industry Advisory Committee to the OECD, and with labour, through the Trade Union Advisory Committee. We have active contacts as well with other civil society organisations. The common thread of our work is a shared commitment to market economies backed by democratic institutions and focused on the wellbeing of all citizens. Along the way, we also set out to make life harder for the terrorists, tax dodgers, crooked businessmen and others whose actions undermine a fair and open society.

str:Agency.com:Name: Metadata Technology
str:Agency.com:Description: Provider of consultancy, software, and services to the SDMX community.

str:Agency.com:Name: International Labour Organization
str:Agency.com:Description:
The ILO was founded in 1919, in the wake of a destructive war, to pursue a vision based on the premise that universal, lasting peace can be established only if it is based on social justice. The ILO became the first specialized agency of the UN in 1946.
The main aims of the ILO are to promote rights at work, encourage decent employment opportunities, enhance social protection and strengthen dialogue on work-related issues.

str:Agency.com:Name: Brazilian Institute of Geography and Statistics (IBGE)
Instituto Brasileiro de Geografia e Estatística
Nigerian National Bureau of Statistics (NBS)
str:Agency.com:Description: The National Bureau of Statistics Nigeria as the mandate to generate, on a continuous and sustainable basis, socio-economic statistics on all facets of development in Nigeria.

str:Agency.com:Name: National Statistical Institute of Guinea-Bissau - Instituto Nacional de Estatística da Guiné-Bissau (INE)
Bank of Israel (BOI)
str:Agency.com:Description: The Bank of Israel is the central bank of the State of Israel. The main objectives of the Bank are listed and prioritized in the Bank of Israel Law : To maintain price stability; to support other objectives of the Government's economic policy, especially growth, employment, and reducing social gaps; and supporting the stability of the financial system.

str:Agency.com:Name: United Nations Statistics Division (UNSD)
str:Agency.com:Description: The Statistics Division of the Department of Economic and Social Affairs of the United Nations Secretariat (UNSD) is committed to the advancement of the global statistical system. UNSD compiles and disseminates global statistical information, develops standards and norms for statistical activities, and supports countries' efforts to strengthen their national statistical systems. UNSD facilitates the coordination of international statistical activities and supports the functioning of the United Nations Statistical Commission as the apex entity of the global statistical system.

str:Agency.com:Name: Inter-agency and Expert Group on SDG Indicators (IAEG-SDGs)
str:Agency.com:Description: The United Nations Statistical Commission created the Inter-agency and Expert Group on SDG Indicators (IAEG-SDGs), composed of Member States and including regional and international agencies as observers. The IAEG-SDGs was tasked to develop and implement the global indicator framework for the Goals and targets of the 2030 Agenda. The global indicator framework was developed by the IAEG-SDGs and agreed upon, including refinements on several indicators, at the 48th session of the United Nations Statistical Commission held in March 2017. The global indicator framework was subsequently adopted by the General Assembly on 6 July 2017 and is contained in the Resolution adopted by the General Assembly on Work of the Statistical Commission pertaining to the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development (A/RES/71/313)

str:Agency.com:Name: United Nations Organization for Education, Science and Culture (UNESCO)
str:Agency.com:Description: UNESCO strives to build networks among nations that enable humanity's moral and intellectual solidarity, by:• Mobilizing for education: so that every child, boy or girl, has access to quality education as a fundamental human right and as a prerequisite for human development.• Building intercultural understanding: through protection of heritage and support for cultural diversity. UNESCO created the idea of World Heritage to protect sites of outstanding universal value.• Pursuing scientific cooperation: such as early warning systems for tsunamis or trans-boundary water management agreements, to strengthen ties between nations and societies.• Protecting freedom of expression: an essential condition for democracy, development and human dignity.Source: http://en.unesco.org/about-us/introducing-unescoThe UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS) is the statistical branch of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (UNESCO). The Institute produces the data and methodologies to monitor trends at national and international levels. It delivers comparative data for countries at all stages of development to provide a global perspective on education, science and technology, culture, and communication.Source: http://www.uis.unesco.org/AboutUIS/Pages/default.aspx

str:Agency.com:Name: Statistical Centre for the Cooperation Council for the Arab Countries of the Gulf
str:Agency.com:Description: Statistical Centre for the Cooperation Council for the Arab Countries of the Gulf

str:Agency.com:Name: Joint Eurostat - ECB maintenance agency role
Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (PULL)

str:ConceptScheme.com:Name: Eurostat (additional) concepts
str:ConceptScheme.com:Description: These concepts are defined in order to be used in Euro-SDMX MSD due to the fact that no relevant concept is defined in the Cross-Domain Concepts Scheme (CDC).

str:Concept.com:Name: Accessibility and clarity
str:Concept.com:Description: The conditions and modalities by which users can obtain, use and interpret data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Accessibility of documentation
str:Concept.com:Description: Accessibility of documentation

str:Concept.com:Name: Accuracy and reliability
str:Concept.com:Description: Accuracy: closeness of computations or estimates to the exact or true values that the statistics were intended to measure Reliability: closeness of the initial estimated value to the subsequent value.

str:Concept.com:Name: Asymmetry for mirror flow statistics - coefficient
str:Concept.com:Description: The difference or the absolute difference of inbound and outbound flows between a pair of countries divided by the average of these two values.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coherence and comparability
str:Concept.com:Description: COHERANCE - Adequacy of statistics to be combined in different ways and for various uses. COMPARABILITY - The extent to which differences between statistics can be attributed to differences between the true values of the statistical characteristics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coherence - sub annual and annual statistics
str:Concept.com:Description: The extent to which statistics of different frequencies are reconcilable

str:Concept.com:Name: Coherence - National Accounts
str:Concept.com:Description: The extent to which statistics are reconcilable with National Accounts.

str:Concept.com:Name: Common units - proportion
str:Concept.com:Description: The proportion of common units covered by both the survey and the administrative sources in relation to the total number of units in the survey.

str:Concept.com:Name: Comparability - domain
str:Concept.com:Description: The extent to which statistics are comparable between statistical domains.

str:Concept.com:Name: Length of comparable time series
str:Concept.com:Description: The number of reference periods in time series from last break.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data completeness - rate
str:Concept.com:Description: The ratio of the number of data cells provided to the number of data cells required.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coverage error
str:Concept.com:Description: Divergence between the frame population and the target population.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data tables - consultations
str:Concept.com:Description: Number of consultations of data tables within a statistical domain for a given time period displayed in a graph.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data revision - average size
str:Concept.com:Description: The average over a time period of the revisions of a key item. The 'revision' is defined as the difference between a later and an earlier estimate of the key item.

str:Concept.com:Name: Imputation - rate
str:Concept.com:Description: The ratio of the number of replaced values to the total number of values for a given variable.

str:Concept.com:Name: Introduction
str:Concept.com:Description: A general description of the statistical process and its outputs, and their evolution over time.

str:Concept.com:Name: Item non-response - rate
str:Concept.com:Description: The ratio of the in-scope (eligible) units which have not responded to a particular item and the in-scope units that are required to respond to that particular item

str:Concept.com:Name: Measurement error
str:Concept.com:Description: Error in reading, calculating or recording numerical value.

str:Concept.com:Name: Metadata completeness - rate
str:Concept.com:Description: The ratio of the number of metadata elements provided to the total number of metadata elements applicable.

str:Concept.com:Name: Metadata - consultations
str:Concept.com:Description: Number of consultations within a statistical domain for a given time period.

str:Concept.com:Name: Model assumption error
str:Concept.com:Description: Error due to domain specific models needed to define the target of estimation.

str:Concept.com:Name: Non response error
str:Concept.com:Description: The difference between the statistics computed from the collected data and those that would be computed if there were no missing values.

str:Concept.com:Name: Over-coverage - rate
str:Concept.com:Description: The proportion of units accessible via the frame that do not belong to the target population.

str:Concept.com:Name: Processing error
str:Concept.com:Description: The error in final data collection process results arising from the faulty implementation of correctly planned information methods.

str:Concept.com:Name: Punctuality - delivery and publication
str:Concept.com:Description: The number of days between the delivery/release date of data and the target date on which they were scheduled for delivery/release.

str:Concept.com:Name: Sampling error - indicators
str:Concept.com:Description: Precision measures for estimating the random variation of an estimator due to sampling.

str:Concept.com:Name: Seasonal adjustment
str:Concept.com:Description: The statistical technique used to remove the effects of seasonal calendar influences operating on a series.

str:Concept.com:Name: Statistical presentation
str:Concept.com:Description: Statistical presentation

str:Concept.com:Name: Statistical processing
str:Concept.com:Description: Statistical processing

str:Concept.com:Name: Time lag - final result
str:Concept.com:Description: The number of days (or weeks or months) from the last day of the reference period to the day of publication of complete and final results.

str:Concept.com:Name: Time lag - first result
str:Concept.com:Description: The number of days (or weeks or months) from the last day of the reference period to the day of publication of first results.

str:Concept.com:Name: Timeliness and punctuality
str:Concept.com:Description: Timeliness and punctuality

str:Concept.com:Name: Unit non-response - rate
str:Concept.com:Description: The ratio of the number of units with no information or not usable information to the total number of in-scope (eligible) units.

str:ConceptScheme.com:Name: Cross Domain Metadata Concepts
str:ConceptScheme.com:Description: Cross-domain concepts in the SDMX framework describe metadata concepts relevant to many statistical domains. SDMX recommends use of the concepts outlined below whenever feasible to promote re-usability and exchange of statistical information and their related metadata between national and international organisations.

str:Concept.com:Name: Accuracy
str:Concept.com:Description: Closeness of computations or estimates to the exact or true values that the statistics were intended to measure.

str:Concept.com:Name: Accuracy - overall
str:Concept.com:Description: Assessment of accuracy, linked to a certain data set or domain, which is summarising the various components into one single measure.

str:Concept.com:Name: Adjustment
str:Concept.com:Description: The set of procedures employed to modify statistical data to enable it to conform to national or international standards or to address data quality differences when compiling specific data sets.

str:Concept.com:Name: Adjustment - coded
str:Concept.com:Description: Type of adjustment used, represented by a code.

str:Concept.com:Name: Adjustment - detail
str:Concept.com:Description: Textual description of the type of adjustment used.

str:Concept.com:Name: Advance notice of changes in methodology
str:Concept.com:Description: Policy on notifying the public of changes in methodology, indicating whether the public is notified before a methodological change affects disseminated data and, if so, how long before.

str:Concept.com:Name: Age
str:Concept.com:Description: The length of time that a person has lived or a thing has existed.

str:Concept.com:Name: Base period
str:Concept.com:Description: The period of time used as the base of an index number, or to which a constant series refers.

str:Concept.com:Name: Civil status
str:Concept.com:Description: Legal, conjugal status of each individual in relation to the marriage laws or customs of the country.

str:Concept.com:Name: Classification system
str:Concept.com:Description: Arrangement or division of objects into groups based on characteristics which the objects have in common.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coherence
str:Concept.com:Description: Adequacy of statistics to be combined in different ways and for various uses.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coherence - internal
str:Concept.com:Description: Extent to which statistics are consistent within a given data set.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coherence - cross domain
str:Concept.com:Description: Extent to which statistics are reconcilable with those obtained through other data sources or statistical domains.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data collection
str:Concept.com:Description: Systematic process of gathering data for official statistics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Provision Agreement Comment
str:Concept.com:Description: Supplementary descriptive text.

str:Concept.com:Name: Dataflow Comment
str:Concept.com:Description: Supplementary descriptive text.

str:Concept.com:Name: Comment
str:Concept.com:Description: Supplementary descriptive text which can be attached to data or metadata

str:Concept.com:Name: Group Comment
str:Concept.com:Description: Supplementary descriptive text.

str:Concept.com:Name: Observation Comment
str:Concept.com:Description: Supplementary descriptive text.

str:Concept.com:Name: Time-Series Comment
str:Concept.com:Description: Supplementary descriptive text.

str:Concept.com:Name: Comparability
str:Concept.com:Description: The extent to which differences between statistics can be attributed to differences between the true values of the statistical characteristics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Comparability - geographical
str:Concept.com:Description: Extent to which statistics are comparable between geographical areas.

str:Concept.com:Name: Comparability - over time
str:Concept.com:Description: Extent to which statistics are comparable or reconcilable over time.

str:Concept.com:Name: Completeness
str:Concept.com:Description: The extent to which all statistics that are needed are available.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data compiling agency
str:Concept.com:Description: The administrative unit compiling the data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Confidentiality
str:Concept.com:Description: A property of data indicating the extent to which their unauthorised disclosure could be prejudicial or harmful to the interest of the source or other relevant parties.

str:Concept.com:Name: Confidentiality - status
str:Concept.com:Description: Information about the confidentiality status of the object to which this attribute is attached.

str:Concept.com:Name: Confidentiality - data treatment
str:Concept.com:Description: Rules applied for treating the data set to ensure statistical confidentiality and prevent unauthorised disclosure.

str:Concept.com:Name: Confidentiality - policy
str:Concept.com:Description: Legislative measures or other formal procedures which prevent unauthorised disclosure of data that identify a person or economic entity either directly or indirectly.

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact
str:Concept.com:Description: Individual or organisational contact points for the data or metadata, including information on how to reach the contact points.

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact email address
str:Concept.com:Description: E-mail address of the contact points for the data or metadata

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact fax number
str:Concept.com:Description: Fax number of the contact points for the data or metadata.

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact person function
str:Concept.com:Description: The area of technical responsibility of the contact, such as "methodology", "database management" or "dissemination".

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact mail address
str:Concept.com:Description: The postal address of the contact points for the data or metadata.

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact name
str:Concept.com:Description: The name of the contact points for the data or metadata.

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact organisation
str:Concept.com:Description: The name of the organisation of the contact points for the data or metadata.

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact phone number
str:Concept.com:Description: The telephone number of the contact points for the data or metadata.

str:Concept.com:Name: Cost and Burden
str:Concept.com:Description: Cost associated with the collection and production of a statistical product and burden on respondents.

str:Concept.com:Name: Cost and Burden - Efficiency management
str:Concept.com:Description: Cost benefit analysis, effectiveness of execution of medium term statistical programs, and ensuring efficient use of resources.

str:Concept.com:Name: Cost and Burden - Resources
str:Concept.com:Description: Staff, facilities, computing resources, and financing to undertake statistical production.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coverage
str:Concept.com:Description: The definition of the population that statistics aim to cover.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coverage - sector
str:Concept.com:Description: Main economic or other sectors covered by the statistics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Coverage - Time
str:Concept.com:Description: The length of time for which data are available.

str:Concept.com:Name: Currency
str:Concept.com:Description: Monetary denomination of the object being measured.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data compilation
str:Concept.com:Description: Operations performed on data to derive new information according to a given set of rules.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data description
str:Concept.com:Description: Main characteristics of the data set described in an easily understandable manner, referring to the data and indicators disseminated.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data presentation
str:Concept.com:Description: Description of the disseminated data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data revision
str:Concept.com:Description: Any change in a value of a statistic released to the public.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data update
str:Concept.com:Description: The last date on which the data element was inserted or modified in the database.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data validation
str:Concept.com:Description: Process of monitoring the results of data compilation and ensuring the quality of the statistical results.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data validation - intermediate
str:Concept.com:Description: Validation that intermediate calculations leading to statistical outputs have been correctly done.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data validation - output
str:Concept.com:Description: Assessment of discrepancies and other problems in statistical outputs.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data validation - source
str:Concept.com:Description: Assessment of discrepancies and other problems related to source data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Decimals
str:Concept.com:Description: The number of digits of an observation to the right of a decimal point.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data dissemination agency
str:Concept.com:Description: The administrative unit that disseminates the data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data presentation - disseminated detail
str:Concept.com:Description: Disseminated subdomain, measure, and time period breakdowns of statistics in the dataset.

str:Concept.com:Name: Dissemination format
str:Concept.com:Description: Media by which statistical data and metadata are disseminated.

str:Concept.com:Name: Dissemination format - other
str:Concept.com:Description: References to the most important other data dissemination done.

str:Concept.com:Name: Documentation on methodology
str:Concept.com:Description: Descriptive text and references to methodological documents available.

str:Concept.com:Name: Education level
str:Concept.com:Description: The highest level of an educational programme the person has successfully completed.

str:Concept.com:Name: Embargo time
str:Concept.com:Description: The exact time at which the data could be made available to the public.

str:Concept.com:Name: Frequency
str:Concept.com:Description: The time interval at which observations occur over a given time period.

str:Concept.com:Name: Frequency of data collection
str:Concept.com:Description: Frequency with which the source data are collected.

str:Concept.com:Name: Frequency detail
str:Concept.com:Description: A further specification of the frequency to include more detailed information about the type of frequency and frequencies not commonly used.

str:Concept.com:Name: Frequency of dissemination
str:Concept.com:Description: The time interval at which the statistics are disseminated over a given time period.

str:Concept.com:Name: Grossing / Netting
str:Concept.com:Description: Form of consolidation used in presenting the data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Index type
str:Concept.com:Description: The type of index number used in the statistical production process.

str:Concept.com:Name: Institutional Mandate
str:Concept.com:Description: Set of rules or other formal set of instructions assigning responsibility as well as the authority to an organisation for the collection, processing, and dissemination of statistics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Institutional Mandate - legal acts and other agreements
str:Concept.com:Description: Legal acts or other formal or informal agreements that assign responsibility as well as the authority to an agency for the collection, processing, and dissemination of statistics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Institutional Mandate - data sharing
str:Concept.com:Description: Arrangements or procedures for data sharing and coordination between data producing agencies.

str:Concept.com:Name: Institutional Mandate - respondent relations
str:Concept.com:Description: Measures to encourage statistical reporting and/or to sanction non-reporting.

str:Concept.com:Name: Metadata last certified
str:Concept.com:Description: Date of the latest certification provided by the domain manager to confirm that the metadata posted are still up-to-date, even if the content has not been amended.

str:Concept.com:Name: Metadata last update
str:Concept.com:Description: Date of last update of the content of the metadata.

str:Concept.com:Name: Metadata last posted
str:Concept.com:Description: Date of the latest dissemination of the metadata.

str:Concept.com:Name: Metadata update
str:Concept.com:Description: The date on which the metadata element was inserted or modified in the database.

str:Concept.com:Name: Dissemination format - microdata access
str:Concept.com:Description: Information on whether micro-data are also disseminated.

str:Concept.com:Name: Maintenance Agency
str:Concept.com:Description: The agency that maintains domain-specific data structure and metadata structure definitions.

str:Concept.com:Name: Dissemination format - News release
str:Concept.com:Description: Regular or ad-hoc press releases linked to the data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Non-sampling error
str:Concept.com:Description: Error in sample estimates which cannot be attributed to sampling fluctuations.

str:Concept.com:Name: Observation Pre-break Value
str:Concept.com:Description: The observation, at a time series break period, that was calculated using the old methodology.

str:Concept.com:Name: Observation Status
str:Concept.com:Description: Information on the quality of a value or an unusual or missing value.

str:Concept.com:Name: Observation Value
str:Concept.com:Description: The value, at a particular period, of a particular variable.

str:Concept.com:Name: Occupation
str:Concept.com:Description: The definition of an occupation covers various jobs or positions which are held by individual workers who perform one or another of the possible combinations of the tasks described.

str:Concept.com:Name: Dissemination format - online database
str:Concept.com:Description: Information about on-line databases in which the disseminated data can be accessed.

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact organisation unit
str:Concept.com:Description: An addressable subdivision of an organisation

str:Concept.com:Name: Originator Data identifier
str:Concept.com:Description: The data identifier as found in the originating database.

str:Concept.com:Name: Professionalism
str:Concept.com:Description: Standard expected from a person who is well trained in a particular job.

str:Concept.com:Name: Professionalism - code of conduct
str:Concept.com:Description: Provision for assuring the qualifications of staff and allowing staff to perform their functions without intervention motivated by non-statistical objectives.

str:Concept.com:Name: Professionalism - impartiality
str:Concept.com:Description: Description of the elements providing assurances that statistics are produced on an impartial basis.

str:Concept.com:Name: Professionalism - methodology
str:Concept.com:Description: Describes the elements providing assurances that the choices of sources and statistical techniques as well as decisions about dissemination are informed solely by statistical considerations.

str:Concept.com:Name: Professionalism - statistical commentary
str:Concept.com:Description: Describes the elements providing assurances that the statistical entity is entitled to comment on erroneous interpretation and misuse of statistics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Dissemination format - Publications
str:Concept.com:Description: Regular or ad-hoc publications in which the data are made available to the public.

str:Concept.com:Name: Punctuality
str:Concept.com:Description: Time lag between the actual delivery of the data and the target date when it should have been delivered.

str:Concept.com:Name: Quality management - assessment
str:Concept.com:Description: Overall assessment of data quality, based on standard quality criteria.

str:Concept.com:Name: Quality assurance
str:Concept.com:Description: Guidelines focusing on quality in general and dealing with quality of statistical programmes, including measures for ensuring the efficient use of resources.

str:Concept.com:Name: Quality management - documentation
str:Concept.com:Description: Documentation on procedures applied for quality management and quality assessment.

str:Concept.com:Name: Quality management
str:Concept.com:Description: Systems and frameworks in place within an organisation to manage the quality of statistical products and processes.

str:Concept.com:Name: Recording basis
str:Concept.com:Description: Processes and standards employed in calculating statistical aggregates.

str:Concept.com:Name: Reference area
str:Concept.com:Description: The country or geographic area to which the measured statistical phenomenon relates.

str:Concept.com:Name: Reference period - Collection
str:Concept.com:Description: Dates or periods during which observations have been collected (such as middle, average or end of period) to compile the indicator for the target reference period.

str:Concept.com:Name: Reference Period
str:Concept.com:Description: The period of time or point in time to which the measured observation is intended to refer.

str:Concept.com:Name: Reference period - Target
str:Concept.com:Description: The period that the data is designed to represent.

str:Concept.com:Name: Reference period - Weights
str:Concept.com:Description: Dates or periods to which the observations used to compile the weights refer.

str:Concept.com:Name: Relevance
str:Concept.com:Description: The degree to which statistical information meets the real or perceived needs of clients.

str:Concept.com:Name: Release calendar access
str:Concept.com:Description: Access to the release calendar information.

str:Concept.com:Name: Release calendar
str:Concept.com:Description: The schedule of statistical release dates.

str:Concept.com:Name: Release policy - policy commentary
str:Concept.com:Description: Whether or not ministerial commentary is provided on the occasion of statistical release.

str:Concept.com:Name: Release policy
str:Concept.com:Description: Rules for disseminating statistical data to interested parties.

str:Concept.com:Name: Release policy - legal acts and other agreements
str:Concept.com:Description: Legal acts and other agreements pertaining to data access.

str:Concept.com:Name: Release policy - transparency
str:Concept.com:Description: Dissemination of the release policy to the public.

str:Concept.com:Name: Release policy - user access
str:Concept.com:Description: The policy for release of the data to users, the scope of dissemination (eg, to the public), how users are informed that the data are being released, and whether the policy provides for the dissemination of statistical data to all users at the same time. It also describes the policy for briefing the press in advance of the release of the data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Reporting agency
str:Concept.com:Description: The administrative unit that supplies the data for a given instance of the statistics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data revision - policy
str:Concept.com:Description: Policy aimed at ensuring the transparency of disseminated data, whereby preliminary data are compiled that are later revised.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data revision - practice
str:Concept.com:Description: Information on the data revision practice.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data revision - studies
str:Concept.com:Description: Information about data revision studies and analyses.

str:Concept.com:Name: Sampling
str:Concept.com:Description: The process of selecting a number of cases from all the cases in a particular group or universe.

str:Concept.com:Name: Sampling error
str:Concept.com:Description: That part of the difference between a population value and an estimate thereof, derived from a random sample, which is due to the fact that only a subset of the population is enumerated.

str:Concept.com:Name: Sex
str:Concept.com:Description: This concept is applied if data needs to be categorised by sex, where sex is "the state of being male or female".

str:Concept.com:Name: Source data
str:Concept.com:Description: Characteristics and components of the raw statistical data used for compiling statistical aggregates.

str:Concept.com:Name: Statistical concepts and definitions
str:Concept.com:Description: Statistical characteristics of statistical observations.

str:Concept.com:Name: Statistical population
str:Concept.com:Description: The total membership or population or "universe" of a defined class of people, objects or events.

str:Concept.com:Name: Statistical unit
str:Concept.com:Description: Entity for which information is sought and for which statistics are ultimately compiled.

str:Concept.com:Name: Supplementary data
str:Concept.com:Description: Data not routinely disseminated that are made available to users upon request.

str:Concept.com:Name: Timeliness
str:Concept.com:Description: Length of time between data availability and the event or phenomenon they describe

str:Concept.com:Name: Time format
str:Concept.com:Description: Technical format in which time is represented for the measured phenomenon.

str:Concept.com:Name: Timeliness - Output
str:Concept.com:Description: The lapse of time between the end of a reference period (or a reference date) and dissemination of the data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Time period
str:Concept.com:Description: The period of time or point in time to which the measured observation refers.

str:Concept.com:Name: Timeliness - Source data
str:Concept.com:Description: The time between the end of a reference period (or a reference date) and actual receipt of the data by the compiling agency.

str:Concept.com:Name: Title
str:Concept.com:Description: Textual label used as identification of a statistical object.

str:Concept.com:Name: Unit of measure
str:Concept.com:Description: The unit in which the data values are measured.

str:Concept.com:Name: Unit of measure detail
str:Concept.com:Description: Additional textual information on the unit of measure.

str:Concept.com:Name: Unit Multiplier
str:Concept.com:Description: Exponent in base 10 specified so that multiplying the observation numeric values by 10^UNIT_MULT gives a value expressed in the UNIT.

str:Concept.com:Name: Relevance - User Needs
str:Concept.com:Description: Description of users and their respective needs with respect to the statistical data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Relevance - User Satisfaction
str:Concept.com:Description: Measures to determine user satisfaction.

str:Concept.com:Name: Valuation
str:Concept.com:Description: The definition of the price per unit, for goods and services flows and asset stocks.

str:Concept.com:Name: Counterpart reference area
str:Concept.com:Description: The secondary area (as opposed to "reference area") to which the measured data is in relation.
